description: Linear left-to-right pipeline showing all six stages of optical switch signal processing: LED emits light (stage 1) → beam travels through switch housing (stage 2) → phototransistor receives or does not receive light (stage 3) → analog-to-digital converter samples the PT output (stage 4, terracotta outline) → firmware applies a threshold to the digital reading each polling cycle (stage 5, terracotta outline) → keypress event is dispatched (stage 6). Stages 4 and 5 are highlighted in terracotta because they represent the computational path where polling latency is introduced. Sub-labels below stages 4 and 5 read 'analog → digital' and 'polling interval' respectively.

data_sources.content: article brief and thock bearings.md style guide
